description.task: Model a Spring Boot application's startup sequence. Simulate what happens when SpringApplication.run() is called: context initialization, bean registration, and server startup.
instructions: Then call ctx.registerBean() for 3 simulated beans: 'mySpringBootApp', 'dispatcherServlet', 'dataSource'.
Print 'Started MySpringBootApp in 2.3 seconds' at the end of run().
description.hint: When you call SpringApplication.run(), Spring Boot initializes the full context, discovers beans, auto-configures, and starts the embedded server automatically.
description.expectedOutput:
.   ____          _            __ _ _
 :: Spring Boot :: (v3.2.0)
Bean registered: mySpringBootApp
Bean registered: dispatcherServlet
Bean registered: dataSource
Tomcat started on port(s): 8080
Started MySpringBootApp in 2.3 seconds
Application started!

description.task: Create a Java program that scans and describes the package structure of a typical Spring Boot application, identifying controllers, services, repositories, and models by their naming convention.
instructions: Implement detectRole() using if-else or switch with endsWith() checks.
Implement groupByRole() using a LinkedHashMap: iterate classes and group by role.
Output should group all classes correctly by role.
description.hint: Spring Boot's component scan works similarly — it finds @Controller, @Service, @Repository within the base package and registers them as beans.
description.expectedOutput:
Controller:
  com.app.controller.UserController [Controller]
  com.app.controller.ProductController [Controller]
Service:
  com.app.service.UserService [Service]
Repository:
  com.app.repository.UserRepository [Repository]
Entity:
  com.app.model.UserEntity [Entity]
Configuration:
  com.app.config.AppConfig [Configuration]

description.task: Simulate Spring Boot's CommandLineRunner interface — which runs code after application startup. Implement a data seeder that populates an in-memory store when the app starts.
description.context: CommandLineRunner and ApplicationRunner are used in Spring Boot to execute code after the context is loaded. Common uses: data seeding, startup checks, job scheduling.
instructions: DataSeeder.run() adds 'admin', 'alice', 'bob' to userStore and prints 'Data seeding complete.'.
StartupHealthCheck.run() prints 'Checking database connection... OK' and 'Checking cache... OK'.
Output should show health check, seeding, then the user list.
description.hint: In Spring Boot, implement CommandLineRunner or ApplicationRunner and annotate with @Component. Spring will execute all runners after context startup in order.
description.expectedOutput:
Application context loaded. Running startup tasks...
Checking database connection... OK
Checking cache... OK
Data seeding complete.
All startup tasks completed.
Users in store: [admin, alice, bob]
